 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.22}{{1357.5} - {368.09} - {58.01}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{14.22}{931.4}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.02}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {66}) = {1.02}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.02}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {440.32} - {368.09} - {14.22}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {58.01}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{58.01}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {29.0}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{29.0}{13.9}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.09}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{58.01}{{1357.5} - {368.09} - {14.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{58.01}{975.19}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.06}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {66}) = {2.09}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.06}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{48.16}{22.82}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {2.11}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{48.16}{{1433.83} - {16.66} - {912.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{48.16}{504.48}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {2.11}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.1}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {561}  \quad n_{B_1} = {12}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {581}  \quad n_{B_2} = {12}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{561}^2}{12} + \frac{{581}^2}{12}} - \frac{{1142}^2}{24}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{314721}}{12} + \frac{{337561}}{12}} - \frac{{1304164}}{24}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{26226.75} + {28130.08}} - {54340.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {54356.83} - {54340.17}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {16.66}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{16.66}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {16.66}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{16.66}{22.82}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.73}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{{1433.83} - {48.16} - {912.69}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{16.66}{472.98}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {0.73}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.35}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {977.51} - {48.16} - {16.66}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {912.69}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{912.69}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {912.69}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{912.69}{22.82}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {40.0}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{912.69}{{1433.83} - {48.16} - {16.66}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{912.69}{1369.01}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.67}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {20}) = {40.0}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.67}\)

\(T_{A} = {111} \quad SS_{A} = {5.5}\)  \(T_{B} = {116} \quad SS_{B} = {7.33}\)  \(T_{C} = {97} \quad SS_{C} = {2.83}\)  \(T_{D} = {112} \quad SS_{D} = {3.33}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.94}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {24} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {23}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {24} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {20}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7974} - \frac{436^2}{24}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7974} - \frac{190096}{24}\]  \[SS_{total} = {7974} - {7920.67}\]  \[SS_{total} = {53.33}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {5.5 + 7.33 + 2.83 + 3.33}\]  \[SS_{within} = {18.99}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {53.33} - {18.99}\]  \[SS_{between} = {34.34}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{34.34}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {11.45}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{18.99}{20}\]  \[MS_{within} = {0.95}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{11.45}{0.95}\]  \[F_{obt} = {12.05}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{34.34}{53.33}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.64}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {20}) = {12.05}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.64}\)

Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {44842} - \frac{890^2}{18}\]  \[SS_{total} = {44842} - \frac{792100}{18}\]  \[SS_{total} = {44842} - {44005.56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {836.44}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {148.0 + 221.33 + 189.33}\]  \[SS_{within} = {558.66}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {836.44} - {558.66}\]  \[SS_{between} = {277.78}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{277.78}{2}\]  \[MS_{between} = {138.89}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{558.66}{15}\]  \[MS_{within} = {37.24}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{138.89}{37.24}\]  \[F_{obt} = {3.73}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{277.78}{836.44}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.33}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {15}) = {3.73}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.33}\)

Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{627.65}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {209.22}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{567.33}{56}\]  \[MS_{within} = {10.13}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{209.22}{10.13}\]  \[F_{obt} = {20.65}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{627.65}{1194.98}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.53}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {56}) = {20.65}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.53}\)

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.18}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}, df = {12}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{603.43 + 453.71}{6 + 6}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{1057.14}{12}\]  \[s_p^2 = {88.09}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{88.09}{7} + \frac{88.09}{7}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{12.58 + 12.58}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{25.16}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {5.02}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(69.29 - 42.43) - {0}}{5.02}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{26.86}{5.02}\]  \[t_{obt} = {5.35}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(69.29 - 42.43)}{{{\sqrt{88.09}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(26.86)}{9.39}\]  \[d = {2.86}\]

Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{10.21}{8} + \frac{10.21}{8}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.28 + 1.28}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.56}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.6}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(48.75 - 43.25) - {0}}{1.6}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{5.5}{1.6}\]  \[t_{obt} = {3.44}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(48.75 - 43.25)}{{{\sqrt{10.21}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(5.5)}{3.2}\]  \[d = {1.72}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(14) = 3.44, p < 0.01, d = 1.72

The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{3.05}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {12}\) Calculate the standard error  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{133.58}{13}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{10.28}\]  \[s_M = {3.21}\]  calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{s_M}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{49.92 - 35}{3.21}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{14.92}{3.21}\]  \[t_{obt} = {4.65}\]  calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{s}\]  \[d = \frac{49.92 - 35}{11.56}\]  \[d = \frac{14.92}{11.56}\]  \[d = {1.29}\]

State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.82}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {22}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{16.92 + 1.67}{11 + 11}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{18.59}{22}\]  \[s_p^2 = {0.85}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{0.85}{12} + \frac{0.85}{12}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{0.07 + 0.07}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{0.14}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {0.37}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(10.08 - 15.17) - {0}}{0.37}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-5.09}{0.37}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-13.76}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(10.08 - 15.17)}{{{\sqrt{0.85}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(-5.09)}{0.92}\]  \[d = {-5.53}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(22) = -13.76, p < 0.01, d = -5.53

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {2353} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {117181} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {50}\) 
\(T_{A} = {514} \quad SS_{A} = {718.4}\)  \(T_{B} = {444} \quad SS_{B} = {476.4}\)  \(T_{C} = {591} \quad SS_{C} = {570.9}\)  \(T_{D} = {420} \quad SS_{D} = {732.0}\)  \(T_{E} = {384} \quad SS_{E} = {1236.4}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.58}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {50} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {49}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {50} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {45}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {117181} - \frac{2353^2}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {117181} - \frac{5536609}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {117181} - {110732.18}\]  \[SS_{total} = {6448.82}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {718.4 + 476.4 + 570.9 + 732.0 + 1236.4}\]  \[SS_{within} = {3734.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {6448.82} - {3734.1}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2714.72}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{2714.72}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {678.68}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{3734.1}{45}\]  \[MS_{within} = {82.98}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{678.68}{82.98}\]  \[F_{obt} = {8.18}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{2714.72}{6448.82}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.42}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {45}) = {8.18}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.42}\)

title: Practice Problem Generator
text:
Two-Factor ANOVA
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {610} \quad M_{A1B1} = {67.78}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {671.56} \quad n_{A1B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {510} \quad M_{A1B2} = {56.67}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {1004.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {434} \quad M_{A1B3} = {48.22}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {135.56} \quad n_{A1B3} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {497} \quad M_{A2B1} = {55.22}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {153.56} \quad n_{A2B1} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {446} \quad M_{A2B2} = {49.56}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {692.22} \quad n_{A2B2} = {9}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {390} \quad M_{A2B3} = {43.33}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {204.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {9}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {54} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {53}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {54} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {160481} - \frac{2887^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {160481} - \frac{8334769}{54}\)  \(SS_{total} = {160481} - {154347.57}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6133.43}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {671.56 + 1004.0 + 135.56 + 153.56 + 692.22 + 204.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2860.9}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6133.43} - {2860.9}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3272.53}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1554}  \quad n_{A_1} = {27}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1333}  \quad n_{A_2} = {27}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1554}^2}{27} + \frac{{1333}^2}{27}} - \frac{{2887}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2414916}}{27} + \frac{{1776889}}{27}} - \frac{{8334769}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{89441.33} + {65810.7}} - {154347.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {155252.03} - {154347.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {904.46}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{904.46}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {904.46}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{904.46}{59.6}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {15.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{904.46}{{6133.43} - {2228.04} - {140.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{904.46}{3765.36}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {15.18}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.24}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1107}  \quad n_{B_1} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {956}  \quad n_{B_2} = {18}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {824}  \quad n_{B_3} = {18}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1107}^2}{18} + \frac{{956}^2}{18} + \frac{{824}^2}{18}} - \frac{{2887}^2}{54}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1225449}}{18} + \frac{{913936}}{18} + \frac{{678976}}{18}} - \frac{{8334769}}{54}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{68080.5} + {50774.22} + {37720.89}} - {154347.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {156575.61} - {154347.57}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {2228.04}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2228.04}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {1114.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1114.02}{59.6}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {18.69}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2228.04}{{6133.43} - {904.46} - {140.03}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{2228.04}{5088.94}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.44}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {18.69}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.44}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.19}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3272.53} - {904.46} - {2228.04}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {140.03}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{140.03}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {70.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{70.02}{59.6}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {1.17}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{140.03}{{6133.43} - {904.46} - {2228.04}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{140.03}{3000.93}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.05}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {48}) = {1.17}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.05}\)

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {-8.43}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {-59}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {1887}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {1887} - \frac{3481}{7}\]  \[SS_D = {1887} - {497.29}\]  \[SS_D = {1389.71}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{1389.71}{6}\]  \[s_D^2 = {231.62}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{231.62}{7}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{33.09}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {5.75}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-8.43 - 0}{5.75}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{-8.43}{5.75}\]  \[t_{obt} = {-1.47}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{-8.43}{{{\sqrt{231.62}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{-8.43}{15.22}\]  \[d = {-0.55}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(6) = -1.47, p > 0.05, d = -0.55

Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{3.25 - 0}{1.54}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{3.25}{1.54}\]  \[t_{obt} = {2.11}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{3.25}{{{\sqrt{19.07}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{3.25}{4.37}\]  \[d = {0.74}\] 
The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  t(7) = 2.11, p > 0.05, d = 0.74

The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(14) = -5.55, p < 0.05, d = -1.43

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {2239} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {102765} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {50}\) 
\(T_{A} = {470} \quad SS_{A} = {174.0}\)  \(T_{B} = {416} \quad SS_{B} = {270.4}\)  \(T_{C} = {368} \quad SS_{C} = {319.6}\)  \(T_{D} = {503} \quad SS_{D} = {424.1}\)  \(T_{E} = {482} \quad SS_{E} = {105.6}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.89}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {50} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {49}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {50} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {45}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {102765} - \frac{2239^2}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {102765} - \frac{5013121}{50}\]  \[SS_{total} = {102765} - {100262.42}\]  \[SS_{total} = {2502.58}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {174.0 + 270.4 + 319.6 + 424.1 + 105.6}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1293.7}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {2502.58} - {1293.7}\]  \[SS_{between} = {1208.88}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {10} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {9}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {45} - {9}\]  \[df_{error} = {36}\]

Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{4095.02}{{9317.89} - {1657.09}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{4095.02}{7660.8}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.53}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {24}) = {6.89}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.53}\)

The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({3}, {12}) = {4.59}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.53}\)

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{219^2}{5} + \frac{217^2}{5} + \frac{235^2}{5} + \frac{223^2}{5} + \frac{237^2}{5} + \frac{202^2}{5} + \frac{195^2}{5} + \frac{251^2}{5} + \frac{166^2}{5}} - \frac{1945^2}{45}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{47961}{5} + \frac{47089}{5} + \frac{55225}{5} + \frac{49729}{5} + \frac{56169}{5} + \frac{40804}{5} + \frac{38025}{5} + \frac{63001}{5} + \frac{27556}{5}} - \frac{3783025}{45}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{9592.2} + {9417.8} + {11045.0} + {9945.8} + {11233.8} + {8160.8} + {7605.0} + {12600.2} + {5511.2}} - {84067.22}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {85111.8} - {84067.22}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {1044.58}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {4321.78} - {1044.58}\]  \[SS_{error} = {3277.2}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{2146.0}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {536.5}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{3277.2}{32}\]  \[MS_{error} = {102.41}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{536.5}{102.41}\]  \[F_{obt} = {5.24}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{2146.0}{{6467.78} - {1044.58}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{2146.0}{5423.2}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.4}\]
